title: astelia chathamica
intro: Astelia chathamica, also known as the Chatham Island Astelia or Pikopiko, is a species of flowering plant in the lily-of-the-valley family, Asteliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in New Zealand for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The health benefits of Astelia chathamica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and alleviate symptoms of menopause. Herbal preparations of Astelia chathamica are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, and it is also used in traditional Māori medicine as a treatment for various ailments.
